title: Fabric Port-Channel and Member Link Imbalance
exclusions: Standalone **Nexus** fabrics without **APIC** observability; **non-ACI** **port-channels** ingested only as generic **SNMP** **ifHC** counters without **pcAggrIf** context (**other** categories); **pure** **SPAN** or **NetFlow**-only feeds lacking **member_dn** granularity; deep **buffer** **telemetry** **without** byte counters (**separate** **buffer** **UCs**). **Host-side** **NIC** **teaming** imbalances **outside** **ACI** **MO** paths.
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (**rename** per **governance**) blending **five** sourcetypes: **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:interface`** (**inventory**, **oper_state**, **speed**, **member_dn** from **l1PhysIf**/**ethpmPhysIf** JSON); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:pc_stats`** (**tx_bytes**, **rx_bytes**, **po_id** rollups aligned to **pcAggrIf**); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** (**faultInst** for **lacp**, **vpc**, **ethpm** defects); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** (**APIC**/leaf **mnemonics** on **bundles**); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:audit`** (**aaaModLR** **ind** touching **pcAggrIf**/**interface** policies). Primary **MO** polls: **`GET https://<apic>/api/class/pcAggrIf.json`** and **`GET https://<apic>/api/node/mo/<node>/sys/intf/aggr-[<name>].json`** for **member-level** **parity** checks.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) for **`cisco:aci:interface`**, **`cisco:aci:pc_stats`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, and **`cisco:aci:audit`** parsers; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for **`/api/class/pcAggrIf.json`** and **`/api/node/mo/.../sys/intf/aggr-[name].json`** polling; **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) for starter **fabric** views; optional **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) for **VM** **motion** calendars beside **skew** windows.
spl:
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:pc_stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:interface") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval po_id=lower(trim(coalesce(po_id,aggrIfId,pcAggrName,"")))
| eval member_dn=trim(coalesce(member_dn,dn,physIfDn,l1PhysIfDn,""))
| eval tx_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(tx_bytes,bytesTx,txTotal,"0"))
| eval rx_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(rx_bytes,bytesRx,rxTotal,"0"))
| eval speed=tonumber(coalesce(speed,ifSpeed,portSpeed,"0"))
| eval oper_state=lower(trim(coalesce(oper_state,ethpmOperSt,adminSt,"")))
| where isnotnull(po_id) AND len(po_id)>0 AND (match(po_id,"^eth|^po") OR match(member_dn,"aggr-|channel"))
| eval byte_lane=tx_bytes+rx_bytes
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(byte_lane) as sum_b sum(tx_bytes) as stx sum(rx_bytes) as srx latest(speed) as spd latest(oper_state) as opst dc(member_dn) as mbr_tags by _time po_id member_dn node_id fabric_pod
| eventstats sum(sum_b) as po_bytes by _time po_id node_id fabric_pod
| eval member_pct=if(po_bytes>0,round(100*sum_b/po_bytes,2),null())
| eventstats range(member_pct) as pct_spread stdev(member_pct) as pct_jitter dc(mbr_tags) as active_members by _time po_id node_id fabric_pod
| where active_members>=2 AND (pct_spread>=28 OR pct_jitter>=16)
| eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | eval lc=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycleState,lifecycle,"")))
    | eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,code,"")))
    | eval open=if(match(lc,"cleared|resolved") OR match(sev,"cleared"),0,1)
    | where open=1 AND (match(_raw,"pcAggr|ethpm|AggrIf|lacp|port.chan") OR match(lower(_raw),"member|suspend|min.links"))
    | stats count as open_pc_faults dc(sev) as sev_spread
    | eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | eval line=lower(_raw)
    | where match(line,"lacp|port-channel|ethpm|bundle|vpc|member")
    | stats count as syslog_pc_hits
    | eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | eval obj=lower(trim(coalesce(changingObj,ind,aaamodlr_obj,"")))
    | where match(obj,"pcaggr|aggrif|ethpm|l1phys|lacp|ifpol")
    | stats count as audit_if_ops dc(obj) as touched_mos
    | eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
  ]
| table _time fabric_pod node_id po_id member_dn sum_b stx srx spd opst member_pct pct_spread pct_jitter active_members open_pc_faults sev_spread syslog_pc_hits audit_if_ops touched_mos
| sort fabric_pod node_id po_id - member_pct
description: Detects **Cisco ACI** fabric **port-channel** **member** load **imbalance** by comparing **tx_bytes** and **rx_bytes** shares per **member_dn** inside each **po_id**, then **joining** **cisco:aci:fault**, **cisco:aci:syslog**, and **cisco:aci:audit** so **NetOps** can tell **LACP** **convergence**, **policy** **edits**, and **telemetry** **gaps** apart from true **hashing** defects that **starve** individual **spine-leaf** **uplinks**.
value: Keeps **replication** and **HCI** **workloads** off hidden **tail-latency** cliffs by surfacing **oversubscribed** **members** before **drops** alarm, gives **finance**-visible evidence for **uplink** **upgrades**, and shortens **TAC** calls when **skew** arrives with matching **fault** codes rather than **manual** **GUI** **spot** checks alone.
implementation: Deploy **Splunkbase** **4022** lanes plus **1546** HTTPS pulls for **pcAggrIf** and member **MO** JSON, normalize **FIELDALIAS** for **po_id**/**member_dn**, schedule **aci_uc18122_fabric_pc_imbalance_core** every **15m**, and adopt **7777** **macros** after **index** rename; add **4856**/**1810** only when **VMware** calendars explain **asymmetric** bursts.
visualization: **Single-value** **pct_spread** with **sparkline** **member_pct** deltas; **bar** chart **stx** versus **srx** per **member_dn**; **sortable** **table** of **po_id**, **pct_spread**, **pct_jitter**, **join** lane counts, **speed**, **oper_state**.
schema: **Network_Traffic** treats **byte** counters and **po_id**/**member_dn** tuples as **interface**-scoped **traffic** facts; **Performance** aligns **pct_spread**/**pct_jitter** KPIs with **capacity** reviews; **Inventory** alignment uses **oper_state** and **speed** when filtering **suspended** or **mismatched** members.

references.title: Cisco APIC REST API Class Queries (KB)
Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)

detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**

APIC clusters must expose HTTPS on tcp/443 with read-only RBAC sufficient to query **pcAggrIf**, **ethpmPhysIf**, and **l1PhysIf** MOs without granting configuration privileges that disturb live LACP bundles during investigations. Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) and confirm five sourcetypes land in the governed index: **cisco:aci:interface** (inventory and oper states), **cisco:aci:pc_stats** (counters for byte ratios), **cisco:aci:fault** (faultInst narratives), **cisco:aci:syslog** (APIC and leaf mnemonics), and **cisco:aci:audit** (aaaModLR change rows). Normalize FIELDALIAS so Splunk consistently receives **po_id**, **member_dn**, **tx_bytes**, **rx_bytes**, **speed**, and **oper_state** despite vendor JSON drift across APIC releases.

Deploy **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) for GET `https://<apic>/api/class/pcAggrIf.json` on 120–300s cadence. When Visore shows bundle-only attributes, add GET `https://<apic>/api/node/mo/<node>/sys/intf/aggr-[po1].json` pattern calls—replace `<node>` with the target node DN and adjust the bracketed bundle name (for example po1) per fabric—and map payloads into the sourcetype your TA documents for interface versus counter semantics. Align **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) macros with the same index label this UC references. Optional **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) and **Splunk_TA_vmware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) timestamps help separate VM migration windows from fabric hashing defects.

Retain hot storage for at least fourteen days; membered uplinks can emit tens to hundreds of thousands of events daily. Annotate runbooks showing which port-channels carry storage replication or HCI flows so threshold overrides cite workload tier, not raw spread numbers alone.

**Telemetry hygiene** — Ratio searches go blind if **cisco:aci:pc_stats** arrives minutes late while **cisco:aci:interface** stays real-time: operators will chase ghost skew. Keep poll schedules documented beside sourcetype schemas, deduplicate REST payloads that replay identical modTs, and confirm syslog timestamps use consistent time zones. When aaaModLR shows interface edits, expect temporary imbalance while convergence finishes; cross-reference **audit_if_ops** before paging hardware teams.

**Fabric context** — **pcAggrIf** wraps member legs modeled through **ethpmPhysIf** and **l1PhysIf**. LACP and hashing intend even utilization; uneven **tx_bytes** and **rx_bytes** shares waste spine-leaf headroom and inflate tail latency before counters show drops. Splunk focuses on per-member **member_pct** so capacity plans mirror measured traffic, not idealized diagrams.

**Step 1** — Configure data collection

Document modular input hostnames beside APIC CMDB rows to prevent DNS drift masquerading as loss. Prefer rsp-subtree=no on class queries unless engineers require deeper MO trees that spike HTTP volume and trigger 429 throttling. Keep byte counters predominantly in **cisco:aci:pc_stats** and interface state in **cisco:aci:interface** while both remain in index=cisco_aci (rename per policy) so unions stay indexed.

Enable fault ingestion with cleared-state parity across REST and syslog. Stream aaaModLR into **cisco:aci:audit** preserving ind for DNs touching **pcAggrIf**, **l1PhysIf**, lacp policies, or interface profiles edited before skew spikes. Point leaf remote syslog at Splunk with mnemonics covering LACP, vPC, or ethpm transitions. After TA upgrades, sample three members in the APIC GUI against Splunk **speed** and **oper_state**—drift here signals props skew, not optics failure.

**Step 2** — Create the search

Paste the SPL from the spl field, save as **aci_uc18122_fabric_pc_imbalance_core**, wrap indexes in macros if required, then tune **pct_spread** and **pct_jitter** using two weeks of baseline data per pod.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (sourcetype="cisco:aci:pc_stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:interface") earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval po_id=lower(trim(coalesce(po_id,aggrIfId,pcAggrName,"")))
| eval member_dn=trim(coalesce(member_dn,dn,physIfDn,l1PhysIfDn,""))
| eval tx_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(tx_bytes,bytesTx,txTotal,"0"))
| eval rx_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(rx_bytes,bytesRx,rxTotal,"0"))
| eval speed=tonumber(coalesce(speed,ifSpeed,portSpeed,"0"))
| eval oper_state=lower(trim(coalesce(oper_state,ethpmOperSt,adminSt,"")))
| where isnotnull(po_id) AND len(po_id)>0 AND (match(po_id,"^eth|^po") OR match(member_dn,"aggr-|channel"))
| eval byte_lane=tx_bytes+rx_bytes
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(byte_lane) as sum_b sum(tx_bytes) as stx sum(rx_bytes) as srx latest(speed) as spd latest(oper_state) as opst dc(member_dn) as mbr_tags by _time po_id member_dn node_id fabric_pod
| eventstats sum(sum_b) as po_bytes by _time po_id node_id fabric_pod
| eval member_pct=if(po_bytes>0,round(100*sum_b/po_bytes,2),null())
| eventstats range(member_pct) as pct_spread stdev(member_pct) as pct_jitter dc(mbr_tags) as active_members by _time po_id node_id fabric_pod
| where active_members>=2 AND (pct_spread>=28 OR pct_jitter>=16)
| eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | eval lc=lower(trim(coalesce(lc,lifecycleState,lifecycle,"")))
    | eval sev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity,fault_severity,code,"")))
    | eval open=if(match(lc,"cleared|resolved") OR match(sev,"cleared"),0,1)
    | where open=1 AND (match(_raw,"pcAggr|ethpm|AggrIf|lacp|port.chan") OR match(lower(_raw),"member|suspend|min.links"))
    | stats count as open_pc_faults dc(sev) as sev_spread
    | eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | eval line=lower(_raw)
    | where match(line,"lacp|port-channel|ethpm|bundle|vpc|member")
    | stats count as syslog_pc_hits
    | eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
  ]
| join max=1 type=left join_key [
    search index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit" earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | eval obj=lower(trim(coalesce(changingObj,ind,aaamodlr_obj,"")))
    | where match(obj,"pcaggr|aggrif|ethpm|l1phys|lacp|ifpol")
    | stats count as audit_if_ops dc(obj) as touched_mos
    | eval join_key="aci_fabric_pc_imb_22"
  ]
| table _time fabric_pod node_id po_id member_dn sum_b stx srx spd opst member_pct pct_spread pct_jitter active_members open_pc_faults sev_spread syslog_pc_hits audit_if_ops touched_mos
| sort fabric_pod node_id po_id - member_pct
```

**Understanding this SPL** — The union of **cisco:aci:pc_stats** and **cisco:aci:interface** coalesces vendor keys into po_id and member_dn, sums tx_bytes and rx_bytes into byte_lane, and buckets _time at fifteen minutes to absorb REST latency without hiding sustained skew. **member_pct** shows each leg's share of bundle bytes; **eventstats** derives **pct_spread** (range) and **pct_jitter** (standard deviation). Requirements on **active_members** ignore single-leg bundles. Joined summaries for **open_pc_faults**, **syslog_pc_hits**, and **audit_if_ops** prove contextual lanes fired in-window; empty joins highlight telemetry gaps, not automatic fabric health.

**Step 3** — Validate

Compare two alerted bundles against APIC Operational views for matching **node_id** values and confirm **member_pct** tracks GUI counters within one poll interval. Use Visore ethpmPhysIf children to confirm suspended members explain skew before rewriting SPL. Run metadata on sourcetypes for the day and verify recentTime across all five sourcetypes. Align **open_pc_faults** with APIC Faults UI entries referencing lacp, vPC, or ethpm during the same window. Capture change records when **audit_if_ops** spikes align with operator edits.

**Step 4** — Operationalize

Publish per-fabric_pod dashboards with drilldown tokens on **po_id**, **node_id**, and **member_dn** linking to saved Visore filters. Schedule saved searches every fifteen minutes with throttling on fabric_pod, node_id, and po_id to limit duplicate paging. Export weekly CSV summaries of **pct_spread** and **member_pct** into capacity reviews. Mirror alert summaries into ITSM with links to **cisco:aci:audit** rows sharing policy DNs so reviewers see authorized work adjacent to the skew.

**Step 5** — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

**Visualization** — Lead with a single-value of max **pct_spread** across pods plus a sparkline of median **member_pct** deltas. Middle panel: bar chart stacking **stx** vs **srx** per **member_dn** for the selected **po_id**, colorized when **oper_state** is not up. Bottom table lists **po_id**, **node_id**, **active_members**, **pct_spread**, **pct_jitter**, **open_pc_faults**, **syslog_pc_hits**, **audit_if_ops**, **touched_mos**, **speed**, and highlights rows where joins are empty while **pct_spread** violates policy.

**Alert** — Fire when **pct_spread** ≥ 28 across two consecutive fifteen-minute buckets with **active_members** ≥ 2, or when **pct_jitter** ≥ 16 with **open_pc_faults** > 0. Throttle forty-five minutes per fabric_pod, node_id, and po_id. Payloads list the top three **member_dn** values, **member_pct** figures, **sev_spread**, **touched_mos**, **speed**, **oper_state**, and URLs to saved fault and audit investigations. Recovery when **pct_spread** < 18 for four buckets and **oper_state** reads up on all active members.

**Failure modes** — Expired aaaRefresh tokens cause 401/403 responses—repair REST authentication before retuning thresholds. HTTP 429 storms require backoff or follower APIC distribution. Null **member_pct** often means **po_bytes** zeroed—validate FIELDALIAS on **tx_bytes** and **rx_bytes** after upgrades. High **pct_spread** with **active_members** < 2 belongs to bundle health workflows, not ratio alerts. Clock skew between collectors and APIC misaligns GUI checks—verify NTP prior to hardware swaps. Splunkbase 7777 starter content is trustworthy only when macros target the same index as this SPL.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how traffic shares across the cables in a fabric bundle, and we tell you when one line is doing almost all the work while the others stay quiet, so important apps do not slow down without a clear warning.
hardware: Cisco APIC clusters and Cisco Nexus 9000 switches in ACI mode
knownFalsePositives:
**lacp_convergence_pause** — Right after a member returns from link-down or suspend, LACP can park traffic on one leg for tens of seconds while the bundle reconverges. Expect elevated pct_spread for one or two buckets only when syslog shows lacp sync transitions; widen suppression during approved maintenance or require two consecutive violations before paging.

**microburst_smoothing** — Fifteen-minute buckets hide multi-second bursts that spike one member momentarily without persistent imbalance. If micro-bursts dominate, tighten span cautiously (for example ten minutes) only after confirming REST inputs keep up; otherwise you trade false quiet for sharper but noisy math.

**telemetry_dup_poll** — Duplicate REST pulls that replay identical modTs double-count tx_bytes or rx_bytes on paper, falsely narrowing spread. Dedupe on _time, member_dn, and a hashed raw fingerprint, or fix the modular input interval so each poll advances modTs before Splunk indexes another copy.

**asymmetric_workload_design** — Some storage or backup VLANs intentionally pin flows because hosts hash to one neighbor; a stable skew with quiet fault lanes and calm syslog may be normal. Compare against documented traffic engineering and lower severity when audit rows show no interface edits across the observation window.

**apic_http_throttle_gap** — HTTP 429 or brief APIC CPU pressure can delay pc_stats while interface MOs still advance, manufacturing artificial spread for a single interval. Cross-check join health: if pc_stats subsearch volume drops but interface rows remain, pause alerts until REST queues drain.

**issu_counter_reset** — Image upgrades or ISSU can zero counters mid-bucket, collapsing po_bytes and yielding odd member_pct until the next full poll completes. Ignore single-bucket spikes bounded by change records that cite ISSU or policy downloads.

**vpc_diag_traffic** — Diagnostic vPC holds or half-member diagnostic states can bias bytes toward one leg even when forwarding is healthy. Confirm ethpmPhysIf oper states and Cisco TAC bulletins before treating the skew as hashing failure.

**speed_mismatch_member** — Member legs running negotiated speed below siblings shift byte shares legitimately until ports auto-negotiate uniformly. Validate speed and oper_state parity on every member_dn before re-hashing investigations.
